Hull City have announced their intention to appeal against a Football Association charge of failing to control their players during a match against Leicester on March 14. The charge arose from an incident in which Tigers players surrounded referee Jonathan Moss after Alex Bruce was booked for a challenge on Leicester's Riyad Mahrez. Hull City manager Steve Bruce expressed his frustration with the incident, stating that Mahrez was trying to get someone sent off and that the challenge was fair. The appeal will be heard by an independent regulatory commission. The club will vigorously defend the charge. Hull City have been charged with failing to ensure their players conducted themselves in an orderly fashion.

The UCI has announced new anti-doping measures in response to the CIRC report on the Lance Armstrong scandal . Researchers in Australia found that many household products marketed as 'green', 'organic', 'all-natural' and 'non-toxic' contain cancer-causing chemicals. The study analyzed 37 products, including air fresheners, cleaning products, and personal care products, and found that they emitted 156 different volatile organic compounds (VOCs). Of these VOCs, 42 are classified as toxic or hazardous under US federal laws. A nearly 1 million signatures petition calling for the reinstatement of suspended "Top Gear" host Jeremy Clarkson was delivered to the BBC's headquarters in London on Friday. Clarkson was suspended for allegedly hitting a producer, and the show was not broadcast as scheduled Sunday. Clarkson tweeted his thanks to those who signed the petition, saying he is "very touched." The BBC said it will consider the findings of an internal investigation next week and set out any further steps. Clarkson has been at the center of controversy before, including using a racist term during a taping of the show and a "Top Gear" special filmed in Argentina that sparked complaints.

The NHS has spent nearly £350,000 on removing tattoos for people who changed their minds about their body art. Over 2,000 people have received free laser treatment in the past four years. The NHS only provides tattoo removal if it is necessary for health reasons, such as significant distress or mental health problems. However, the true cost is likely to be higher as many trusts did not provide breakdowns of the reasons for laser treatments. The popularity of tattoos has increased due to celebrity fans, but four in ten people regret having a tattoo and one in six want them removed. Private clinics charge around £150 per session for small tattoos

The Aerial Bold project is a crowdsourcing tool that lets people find letters, numbers, and characters in satellite images. The project was set up by German designer Benedikt Groß and geographer Joey Lee. They raised $11,492 on Kickstarter and have since set up the Aerial Bold crowdsourcing tool. The tool lets people scour Mapbox images to find letters, numbers, and characters in the landscape. The project aims to develop a new font and software that can automatically find words and letters on images. The tool uses a grid divided into 21 rectangles, and users must place selection boxes over the relevant parts of the map
